summary:
Implement a function that raises x to the power n, that is, compute x^n, where x is a floating-point number and n is an integer (which may be negative).

A negative exponent means you take the reciprocal, so x^(-k) equals 1 divided by x^k.
constraints: Either x is non-zero or n is greater than 0
The result is guaranteed to fit within a double-precision float (within -10^4 to 10^4)
